name: Nesospingidae
description: Nesospingidae is a monotypic family of passerine bird endemic to the island of Puerto Rico. The single species is a medium-sized tanager found in montane forests and is the only member of its family.

children.name: Nesospingus
children.description: A monotypic genus of tanager endemic to Puerto Rico's montane forests.

children.name: Nesospingus speculiferus
children.description: The Puerto Rican Tanager is an olive-brown bird with a white patch on the wing and a pale eye. It inhabits the canopy of montane rainforests in Puerto Rico, foraging for fruit and insects.
